note: This file documents the credentials that must be configured in the n8n UI. These are NOT importable - you must create each credential manually in n8n Settings > Credentials.

credentials.name: PuraEstate PostgreSQL
credentials.setup_notes: Create in n8n: Settings > Credentials > New > PostgreSQL

credentials.name: PuraEstate Slack

fields.token: xoxb-XXXX (Bot Token from your Slack App)

setup_notes: 1. Go to https://api.slack.com/apps
2. Create New App > From Scratch
3. App Name: PuraEstate Bot, Workspace: your workspace
4. Go to OAuth & Permissions > Bot Token Scopes
5. Add scopes: channels:join, channels:read, chat:write, chat:write.public
6. Install App to Workspace
7. Copy Bot User OAuth Token (starts with xoxb-)

8. Create channels: #property-updates, #new-listings, #price-drops, #alerts, #daily-listings, #market-reports, #agent-performance, #data-quality, #ops-alerts, #inbox-listings, #webhook-ingestion, #hot-leads, #follow-ups, #integrations, #reports
9. Invite bot to each channel: /invite @PuraEstateBot

credentials.name: PuraEstate SMTP
setup_notes: For Gmail: Enable 2FA on Google Account
Go to Google Account > Security > App Passwords
Create App Password for 'Mail'
Use that 16-char password in n8n

credentials.name: PuraEstate IMAP listings@puraestate.co.za
credentials.setup_notes: Same Gmail App Password process as SMTP

credentials.name: PuraEstate Google Sheets
setup_notes: 1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com
2. Create new project: PuraEstate
3. Enable APIs: Google Sheets API, Google Drive API
4. Go to Credentials > Create Credentials > OAuth 2.0 Client ID
5. Application type: Web Application
6. Authorized redirect URIs: https://n8n.puraestate.co.za/rest/oauth2-credential/callback
7. Copy Client ID and Client Secret to n8n
8. In n8n, click Connect and authenticate with your Google account
9. Create a Google Sheet with tabs: 'Properties' and 'Summary'
10. Copy the Sheet ID from the URL and set GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_PROPERTIES

credentials.name: PuraEstate AWS
setup_notes: 1. Go to AWS IAM Console
2. Create new user: puraestate-n8n-backup
3. Attach policy: AmazonS3FullAccess (or create custom policy for specific bucket)
4. Create Access Key and note credentials
5. Create S3 bucket: puraestate-backups in af-south-1
6. Enable versioning on the bucket
7. Set up lifecycle rule: move to Glacier after 30 days, delete after 365 days

environment_variables.description: These variables must be set in n8n's environment before starting
environment_variables.method: Add to /etc/systemd/system/n8n.service [Service] section or to docker-compose.yml environment section
